content.full_text:
Sirs,

     I am writing this letter to determine my eligibility to
discuss and vote on a proposed project as a Planning Commissioner
in the City of Encinitas.

     The proposed project will start with a public hearing before
the commission to provide input into an Environmental Impact
Report.  The commission will then conduct a hearing to certify the
E.I.R. and either at that hearing or a later one, will approve one
of the alternatives addressed in the E.I.R.

     The project is titled "Encinitas General Plan Amendment,
Highway 680 Deletion and Alternatives."  I am enclosing an excerpt
from the Draft E.I.R. that gives a history of the project.

     I live in the community of Olivenhain through which the
original 680 alignment runs.  I live in a subdivision with 1 acre+
lots which contains a portion of this alignment.  My home is 700
feet from the slope rights easement along the alignment, 780 feet
to the road's centerline, and 160 feet higher in elevation.

     The road will provide much greater accessibility to my
neighborhood making it a more convenient place to live but will
also bring through traffic.  I feel that I can be objective and
unbiased when participating as a commissioner but do not want to
jeopardize the process for the city.  I therefore request that you
determine my eligibility the participate in this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Charles DuVivier

1.0 INTRODUCTION

1.1 Purpose

This document is a supplement to the Final Environmental Impact Report (FEIR) for
the Encinitas General Plan and Zoning Ordinance, Encinitas No. 88-001-EIR and
State Clearinghouse No. 8803230? (Cotton/Beland/Associates, 1988). It carries its
own separate Encinitas Case No. 90-211.

The supplement examines deletion of Highway 680 and alternatives to it on the
Encinitas General Plan Circulation Plan. It has been prepared in type and scope
as directed by the Superior Court of the State of California. The document has also
been prepared in compliance with the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA)
of 1970, as amended, and Title 14 of the California Code of Regulations, as revised,
as well as the City of Encinitas Local Guidelines for Implementation of the California
Environmental Quality Act.

According to the California Code of Regulations (Resources Agency, 1991) Title 14,
Div. 6, 6.3 (CEQA Guidelines), Sec. 15162, a supplement provides a means for
utilizing an Environmental Impact Report (EIR) under certain conditions when
changes occur. The conditions include subsequent changes proposed in a project
which will require important revisions in the EIR, substantial changes occurring
in the circumstances under which the project is undertaken which will require
important revisions to the EIR, or new information becoming available. This
information may reveal increased severity of significant effects, new significant
effects, change in the feasibility and effectiveness of mitigation measures or
alternatives, or new mitigation measures or alternatives.

Title 14, Div 6, 6.3, Sec. 15163 provides that a supplement rather than a subsequent
EIR can be prepared if only minor additions or changes are necessary to make the
previous EIR apply to the project in the changed situation. The supplement to the
EIR need contain only the information necessary to make the previous EIR adequate
for the project as revised. The supplement must be given the same kind of notice
and public review as given to the draft EIR. However, the supplement may be
circulated by itself without recirculating the previous EIR. When the agency
decides whether to approve the project, the decision-making body must consider
the previous EIR as revised by the supplement and a finding under Sec. 15091
made for each significant effect shown in the previous EIR as revised.

The FEIR prepared for the Encinitas General Plan and Zoning Ordinance examined
two different circulation networks under one land use scenario. A subsequent
addendum examined three additional circulation networks not previously evaluated
in the FEIR under two land use scenarios. All three networks included
classification and extension of Leucadia Boulevard.

1.2 Background

According to public records (Routes and Right-of-ways, 1991) Highway 680 first
appeared on the San Diego County General Plan Circulation Element in 1964. It was
classified as a Prime Arterial with an alignment running from the Coast Highway in
Encinitas easterly to Del Dios Highway and southeasterly on Lakeside. This

1

alignment overlaid El Camino del Norte through Rancho Santa Fe. In 1967 the
segment through Rancho Santa Fe was moved north to its present alignment.

Highway 680 then remained unchanged on the Circulation Element until 1977, when
the segment between the Coast and Rancho Bernardo Road was deleted. The next
year this segment was reinstated. In 1979 the segment between Del Dios Highway
and Black Mountain Road was deleted. Highway 680 was made whole again in mid
1980. However, later that year the segment from the Coast to Rancho Bernardo
Road was deleted again. Highway 680 was third whole for the third time in 1981,
and at the same time downgraded to a Major Road. In 1988 the segment between
the newly incorporated City of Encinitas eastern boundary and Del Dios Highway
was further downgraded to a Collector Road.

Upon incorporation in 1986, the City of Encinitas began formulation of a new
comprehensive General Plan for the City which was adopted in 1988. Highway 680
was removed from the Circulation Plan and underlying roads newly classified.
Along the western alignment Leucadia Boulevard between the Coast Highway and
Interstate 5 was classified as an Augmented Local Street. It was also extended
from I-5 to El Camino Real as a Major Road with a "Scenic Roadway" designation
and an 85 foot right-of-way. Olivenhain Road between El Camino Real and Rancho
Santa Fe Road was classified as a Primary Arterial. Rancho Santa Fe Road was
classified as a Augmented Local Street. Finally, the eastern segment of Highway
680 between Rancho Santa Fe Road and the City's boundary was deleted entirely.

Shortly after adoption of the Encinitas General Plan, the Rancho Santa Fe
Association filed a lawsuit in the Superior Court of the State of California (Case No.
N43568) challenging the adequacy under CEQA of the City's procedures and
treatment in deleting Highway 680 east of Rancho Santa Fe Road from the
Circulation Plan. In 1990 the Court ordered reinstatement of Highway 680 on the
Circulation Plan and directed the City to prepare a supplement to the General Plan
EIR addressing traffic and other environmental issues related to deletion of the
highway.

In addition, the Court directed that the supplement specifically consider impacts
to El Camino del Norte through Rancho Santa Fe if Highway 680 is deleted. The
Court also required analysis of reasonable project alternatives. Formal scoping of
the supplement resulted in a range of alternatives from maintaining Highway 680
as a Major Road, through reclassification of Highway 680 as Collector and Local
roadways, to deletion of Highway 680 together with institution of three alternate
roadways.

1.3 Content

The format of this supplement is similar to that of the FEIR and its addenda. The
introduction is followed by a summary (Section 2) which includes the environmental
setting, a description of the project and alternatives, and potential significant
effects and mitigation measures of the project and alternatives. The summary
includes a matrix comparing the project and alternatives.

The environmental setting of the project and alternatives (Section 3) is then
described in detail. This section is followed by a description of the project and
alternatives (Section 4) including locations and individual characteristics. An

2

The proposed project will start with a public hearing before the commission to provide input into an Environmental Impact Report. The commission will then conduct a hearing to certify the E.I.R. and either at that hearing or a later one, will approve one of the alternatives addressed in the E.I.R. The project is titled "Encinitas General Plan Amendment, Highway 680 Deletion and Alternatives." I am enclosing an excerpt from the Draft E.I.R. that gives a history of the project. I live in the community of Olivenhain through which the original 680 alignment runs. I live in a subdivision with 1 acre+ lots which contains a portion of this alignment. My home is 700 feet from the slope rights easement along the alignment, 780 feet to the road's centerline, and 160 feet higher in elevation. The road will provide much greater accessibility to my neighborhood making it a more convenient place to live but will also bring through traffic. I feel that I can be objective and unbiased when participating as a commissioner but do not want to jeopardize the process for the city. I therefore request that you determine my eligibility to participate in this matter.
